skill.name: professional-communication
skill.description: Helps software developers write clear professional messages, emails, meeting notes, status updates, and stakeholder communications.
skill.summary: Practical communication frameworks for emails, team messages, meetings, and technical audience adaptation.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static findings to be false positives caused by markdown fences, inline code formatting, and ordinary communication terminology. No runnable scripts, command execution, network activity, filesystem access, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Dismissed External Command Detections
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the locations are markdown code fences, inline code spans, file references, or message examples. No executable script, shell invocation, or user-controlled command path is present. Confidence: 0.98. Confidence reasoning: All reviewed locations are documentation examples or formatting markers in markdown files. The skill only allows Read, Glob, and Grep in SKILL.md and contains no runnable code files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All reviewed locations are documentation examples or formatting markers in markdown files. No shell API or executable code path exists.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Detections
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithm patterns, but the cited lines contain professional communication prose, placeholders, or general technology explanations. No MD5, SHA-1, cipher setup, hashing call, credential handling, or cryptographic implementation is present. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: The reviewed lines discuss communication and software concepts in plain language. There is no code that performs cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are prose and template text. There is no cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm invocation.

low_findings.title: Dismissed System Reconnaissance Detections
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance terms, but the cited lines refer to communication behavior, meeting questions, audience guidance, or scheduling advice. No commands collect host, user, network, environment, or process information. Confidence: 0.96. Confidence reasoning: The terms appear in normal workplace communication examples. No reconnaissance command or data collection workflow is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms appear in meeting and communication guidance. No host inspection, network inspection, or environment collection exists.

content.value_statement: Software teams lose time when messages are vague, too technical, or hard to act on. This skill provides clear structures for emails, chat, meetings, and stakeholder updates.
actual_capabilities: Structures professional emails with subjects, context, clear asks, and next steps.
Improves team chat messages for async work and faster responses.
Creates meeting agendas, summaries, action items, and review structures.
Adapts technical explanations for managers, customers, and cross-functional teams.
Provides templates for status updates, escalations, review requests, and follow-ups.
Simplifies developer jargon while preserving the main technical meaning.
limitations: Does not send messages or connect to email, chat, or calendar tools.
Does not verify facts, project status, deadlines, or stakeholder names.
Does not replace legal, HR, or crisis communication review.
Requires the user to provide context, audience, tone, and desired outcome.

use_cases.title: Write Clear Status Updates
use_cases.description: Turn rough progress notes into a concise update with progress, blockers, risks, and next steps.

use_cases.title: Prepare Effective Meetings
use_cases.description: Create agendas, decision formats, and summaries for planning, retrospectives, reviews, and postmortems.

use_cases.title: Translate Technical Topics
use_cases.description: Explain architecture, incidents, performance work, or security topics in plain language for non-technical audiences.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Email
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a professional email to [audience] about [topic]. Include the main point, context, requested action, and deadline.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Team Message
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this team chat message for clarity and async work. Keep it concise, direct, and action-oriented: [message].

prompt_templates.title: Create a Meeting Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a meeting agenda for [meeting type]. Include objective, agenda items, preparation, expected outcome, and follow-up summary format.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt Technical Communication
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt this technical explanation for [audience]. Preserve accuracy, remove unnecessary jargon, and lead with business impact: [technical text].

output_examples.input: Rough note: The release is late because QA found a payment bug. Need stakeholders to know the new date.
output_examples.output:
Subject: Checkout Release Update and Revised Timeline

The checkout release will move from March 8 to March 15. QA found a payment issue that needs full regression testing.

Next steps: QA will complete retesting by Thursday. I will send a confirmed release update on Friday.

output_examples.input: Rough chat: Hey, are you around? I have a deploy problem.
output_examples.output: Hi Sarah. I am blocked on the staging deploy. The script returns a permission error on line 42. Have you seen this before?

output_examples.input: Technical text: We are adding Redis caching to reduce database load and improve latency.
output_examples.output: We are making key pages load faster by storing frequently used data in memory. This reduces strain on the database during busy periods.

best_practices: Provide audience, purpose, deadline, and desired action before asking for a draft.
Keep the main point first, then add context and supporting details.
Review names, dates, links, and factual claims before sending any message.
anti_patterns: Sending template text without adapting it to the audience and relationship.
Hiding the request or decision needed at the end of a long message.
Using technical detail when the reader only needs impact, options, and next steps.

faq.question: Can this skill write complete emails?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft emails when you provide the audience, topic, context, and requested action.

faq.question: Does it support chat messages?
faq.answer: Yes. It helps write concise Slack, Teams, or Discord messages for async collaboration.

faq.question: Can it create meeting agendas?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes structures for standups, planning, retrospectives, reviews, postmortems, and one-on-one meetings.

faq.question: Can it simplify technical jargon?
faq.answer: Yes. It translates common engineering terms into plain language for stakeholders and customers.

faq.question: Will it send messages for me?
faq.answer: No. It only helps draft and improve communication. You must send messages through your own tools.

faq.question: Is it only for software developers?
faq.answer: No. The examples focus on software teams, but the frameworks apply to many professional settings.
